chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Hello all, welcome to the video lecture series of optical communication.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Subject code is REC 075 and here we are in lecture number 8.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So what we have covered in the previous lecture, so we are talking about basically electromagnetic

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: wave theory, EM theory inside your optical waveguide.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: In which we have discussed about EM wave, then we have discussed about the modes, modes

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: in a planar waveguide, which we have discussed in the last lecture like M is equal to 0,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: that means what will happen at T0, what will happen at T1, what will happen at T2, what

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: will happen at T3, there are different types of modes.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That depends upon the reflection between the phase change between the two points, between

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: the successive two reflections.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And then we have discussed about the phase velocity, that is the velocity of your light

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: ray inside the optical fiber.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now in this lecture what we are going to discuss, the another main topic we have is your group

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: velocity and the next one is the evanescence field.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So let's start, first of all group velocity.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So as you must have read group velocity earlier, what was group velocity, phase velocity?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: The velocity of the wave inside your optical fiber.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: What is group velocity?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: The velocity when the wave travels, what will it get after that?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Phase change of 2pi by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: If we get a complete phase change of 2pi radian, where the phase change is obtained, that velocity

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: is called group velocity.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So if I have to derive the formula of group velocity, if you remember, like we have seen

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: the vp, vp is omega by beta, so vg is d omega by d beta.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So vg is equal to d omega by d beta.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: The same formula is here, group velocity vg is defined as d omega by d beta.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now what can I write d omega by d beta?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: I can divide and multiply both the sides by d lambda and d lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this will become d lambda by d beta and d omega by d lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So we will solve both of them separately.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Then we get the final value of the group velocity.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So let us first see d lambda by d omega by d lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So as we know that lambda is equal to what?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Lambda is equal to c by f.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: What is the value of lambda?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: c by f.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So if I solve f from here, what will be the value of f?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: c by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now if you multiply both the sides by 2pi f, multiply 2pi here and multiply 2pi here

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So what is this term?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Omega.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this becomes omega is equal to 2pi c by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So what did I do?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: I started with lambda because we know that lambda is equal to c by f.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So I took out the value of f from here and in that f, we have taken the value of omega

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: which is 2pi f.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So you can directly say that omega is related to your lambda and given as 2pi c by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now what did I have to calculate?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: I had to calculate d omega by d lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So now I will differentiate this function omega in terms of lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So what will be d omega by d lambda?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This 2pi c is a constant term.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And what will be 1 by lambda?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: 1 by lambda will be lambda to the power minus 1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: If you differentiate this, then minus 1 lambda to the power minus 2.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That means minus 1 by lambda square.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So we will get 2pi c by lambda square.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now if we write this c again from this formula that c is equal to lambda f.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So if you convert lambda f here, then 1 lambda will be cut from 1 lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And this will again become 2pi f.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So 2pi f is again omega.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So ultimately if I talk, then the value of d omega by d lambda will be equal to minus

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: omega by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Got it?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Solve it?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So the value of d omega by d lambda that we had to keep here, we will keep here minus

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: omega by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now similarly, the second value that I have to calculate, I have to do it by differentiating

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: lambda in terms of beta.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So we saw in the first lecture that what was the formula for beta?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Beta is equal to n1 2pi by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now if we solve d beta by d lambda from here, then what we will do later?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: d lambda by d beta.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That means we will inverse it.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So we differentiate d beta in terms of lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So let's do it.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now this is, now see here n1 is there.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: 2pi is a constant term, but there is a term n1 and there is a term lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now we are differentiating in terms of lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: If you remember, I had told you one thing in the last lecture, when the wave comes under

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: the waveguide, then its refractive index is decreased by a factor lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That means whose function is refractive index?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: It depends on lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That means it is decremented by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So here we consider n1 as dependent on wavelength.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Okay?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So that's why we say refractive index is a function of lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Okay?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now the second thing, so when you differentiate here, then here n1, we have constant here.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: If we differentiate according to lambda, then it becomes minus 2pi by lambda square.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Because 2pi is a constant.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And we saw what is the differentiation of 1 by lambda?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Minus 1 by lambda square.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this term remains constant n1 and the second term becomes minus 2pi by lambda square.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now plus, if you see in the first term, this is n1, so this becomes dn1 by d lambda and

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: 2pi by lambda is constant.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now if from this complete term, 2pi by lambda square is made common with minus, then here

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: you will have n1 and because it was positive here, it will become negative.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: If you have taken lambda square as common, then here it is lambda, so above it is lambda

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: multiplied and dn1 by d lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now this term n1 minus lambda dn1 by d lambda is called, is represent ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Ng is what?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Ng is basically called group index.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Where ng is called n1 minus lambda dn1 by d lambda is called group index.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Okay?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So now we have a value of d beta by d lambda that is equal to minus 2pi by lambda square

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now if I was talking about this, I wanted group velocity, so group velocity is equal

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: to vg is equal to d omega by d beta that can be written as d lambda by d beta multiplied

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: d omega by d lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So that d lambda by d lambda is crossing and what will be left?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: d omega by d beta.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So vg becomes, now I have put the value here, d lambda by d beta, we also solved, we solved

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: d beta by d lambda, d beta by d lambda is equal to minus 2pi by lambda square ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So you reverse it, so it becomes minus lambda square divided by 2pi by 2pi into ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And d omega by d lambda we solved, minus omega by lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So minus will become minus plus, one lambda will cancel one lambda.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And if you write omega here, omega is equal to 2pi f, so from here f, sorry, 2pi will

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: cancel out.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So what will be left?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: f lambda divided by ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And again f lambda is equal to c.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So ultimately vg becomes c by ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That means vg, the group velocity, in light velocity, what factor is it divided by?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: It is divided by ng.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: If you remember the phase velocity, what did we have?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: c by n1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That means our light velocity depends upon the factor, decrease, the velocity inside

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: the fiber, decrease by a factor n1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Okay, so these are the two important values, that is your phase velocity and group velocity

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: with respect to your optical waveguide.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So group velocity is completed.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: After this, now the topic we have to discuss is, this is evidence field.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: But before that, I am again recalling the same diagram here.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That first of all, this is our interface.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This part is your core with a refractive index of m1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And this part is your cladding with a refractive index of n2.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So there is a wave vector occurs, that is your incident wave,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: represented by a, that is given by a0 exponential minus j beta x1x

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: exponential j omega t minus beta z.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Now this is called propagation factor.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: We are assuming that where is the wave propagating?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: In z direction.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And this is called the exponential minus j beta 1x, that is your attenuation factor.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Okay, which I have shown there, e to the power minus alpha z.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So the same x component is available here.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Similarly, one is your transmitted wave.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this is represented by b0.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This is your transmitted wave b0.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So equation of b0 is also written in similar form.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And similarly, there is a reflected ray.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This is your c.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Because as we have seen earlier,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: when any incident ray comes on optical fiber,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: then some part is reflected and some part is reflected back again into the same medium.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So now from this theory,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: if we say before the critical angle for total internal deflection is reached,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: we have already studied the theory that what is the condition of total internal deflection?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That the incidence angle becomes equal to the critical angle.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That is incident angle is equal to phi c.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So if our TIR condition is reached,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: then at that point,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: there is only partial reflection.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: The field in the cladding is of the form of

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: b0 exponential minus j beta x2x exponential j omega t minus beta z.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: But when the total internal deflection occurs,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: if I talk about the earlier condition, then I get the reflection here.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: But as soon as your TIR occurs,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: that is, this incident angle is equal to phi c,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: we will say TIR occurs.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: And when TIR occurs,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: then what will happen to the reflected ray?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: You will not get it.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That is, complete ray is transmitted back.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That is, totally we got the reflection.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Which is our TIR condition.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So when TIR condition occurs,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: then we are saying that this beta x2 becomes imaginary

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: and may be written as j minus j epsilon 2.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: That is, if I put value instead of this,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: then it will be minus j epsilon of 2 x.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So minus minus will make you positive.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: But here is j and j, so this will again minus 1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this will return as like this,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: exponential minus epsilon 2 x exponential j omega t minus beta z.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So it is clear from here that your amplitude is basically,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: the amplitude of the field in the cladding is observed

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: to decay exponentially due to this term.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Due to this term,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: my field component decreases exponentially

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: in x direction.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Because this is direction of x.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Such a field exhibiting an exponential decaying amplitude

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: is often referred as evidence field.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: What is this thing?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This is your evidence field.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: We said that how does our field propagate?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Like this.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: We are talking about T1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This is basically T1 because there is a 1,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: zero axis interference.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: We can also say zero axis crossing.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: But what is happening is,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: as your field moves towards cladding,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: that is the transmitted ray,

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: what is the transmitted ray doing to itself?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: This will be exponentially decaying.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this is basically called the evidence field.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So this is all about the group velocity

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: and the evanescent field.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: What is group velocity?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: What is the expression we have derived?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Derivation is mostly important.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: It is often asked that

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: what is the group velocity in the case of optical fiber?

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Drive the expression.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So let's finish the lecture with this.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: We will meet in the next lecture

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: where we will summarize unit 1.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Unit 1 will also end.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: So meet you in the next lecture.

chunks.title: Group Velocity & Evanescent Field   Hindi
chunks.text: Thank you.

text: Hello all, welcome to the video lecture series of optical communication. Subject code is REC 075 and here we are in lecture number 8. So what we have covered in the previous lecture, so we are talking about basically electromagnetic wave theory, EM theory inside your optical waveguide. In which we have discussed about EM wave, then we have discussed about the modes, modes in a planar waveguide, which we have discussed in the last lecture like M is equal to 0, that means what will happen at T0, what will happen at T1, what will happen at T2, what will happen at T3, there are different types of modes.
